displayTitle: The Blotter That Makes the Crack Shout

beats.narration: By the developer station the part is already loaded. A flaw a fraction of a thousandth wide still holds penetrant you cannot see.

payload.title: DEVELOPER & DRYING
payload.sub: The step that makes a loaded flaw visible.

beats.narration: Developer is a film of fine white powder, a blotter pressed to the part. Capillary reverses, penetrant bleeds out, and the indication grows wider than the flaw.

payload.heading: Blot it out, wider than the flaw

beats.narration: The film does three jobs: it blots penetrant out, magnifies the indication, and builds contrast — white behind red, brighter glow under UV.
Form sets the order. Aqueous goes on wet, then dry. Dry powder and nonaqueous need a dry part first. Always keep the film thin.

chips.s: Forms b and c, before the oven
Forms a, d, e on a dry surface
Hot or long bakes out the dye

beats.narration: Thin film, right order, gentle heat, then dwell, then read. Too little blots nothing; too much buries the flaw.

payload.sub: Too little blots nothing; too much buries the flaw.
